description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ubuntu:xenial Docker image (with ubuntu:jammy for jammy jobs) and an apt cache stored in apt-cache/ keyed by apt-cache. Define three stages: build, test, and deploy. In the build stage add a build_amd64 job that runs the global before_script then executes `export PYTHON=python3`, `meson build -Db_coverage=true`, `cd build`, and `ninja`, and archives the whole repository as an artifact; also add a build_jammy_amd64 job that uses the ubuntu:jammy image and runs the same script and artifacts. In the test stage add a test_amd64 job depending on build_amd64 that runs `cd build`, `chmod a+rw -R .`, `sudo -u tester ninja test`, `sudo -u tester ninja coverage-html`, and `sudo ninja install`, publishing the repository as an artifact, and a test_jammy_amd64 job using ubuntu:jammy with identical commands depending on build_jammy_amd64. Finally, add a pages job in the deploy stage that runs only on the master branch, depends on test_amd64, creates a public directory, copies the coverage report and documentation into it, and publishes the public/ folder as a GitLab Pages artifact.
